dialogueLines.speaker: Story_Arachne_01
dialogueLines.text: ...My point, Arachne, is... I've no intention to deflect blame for what I've done. And although I cannot rescind my actions, know that your safety in these woods is guaranteed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Arachne_01
dialogueLines.text: I do appreciate your candor in this, Titaness. However, if my safety now is your concern... you can begin by showing yourself out. With all due respect.

dialogueLines.speaker: Story_Arachne_01
dialogueLines.text: Very well. I shan't intrude upon your home again; good evening.

dialogueLines.speaker: Story_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Look at me when I'm talking to you, you louse. Oh, right. You can't. Must be so sick of staring at yourself by now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: The only thing I'm sick of, I-Forget-Your-Name, is you tramping about, disturbing my peace! If you don't have anything nice to say, why don't you go?

dialogueLines.speaker: Story_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Still indignant after all this time. Had you learned to show respect, you might not be stuck forever in the company of a buffoon. Well, drink it in.

dialogueLines.speaker: Story_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Tell me again I'm playing hard-to-get, and so help me, I will pluck your limbs from you one at a time like they're the petals of an itty bitty flower. How's that sound?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sounds bad, that's how! But also like something somebody playing hard-to-get would say. So I don't know! Now why don't you... tell me some more about yourself?

dialogueLines.speaker: Story_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait, you want me to stay. Something's come over you, all right. Well, clear enough that my work here is done. Know what's good for you, then don't go breaking any other hearts.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
endLines.text: Oh yeah. She wants you, man. That much is very clear!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Prepare to suffer for all that you've done, wretch! Or shall I stay my hand merely because you made the mistake of loosening your hold on me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...All of these... newfound thoughts... within my mind...! Was all of this your doing, witch? I... have no memory of you, and yet.... Who are you...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Chronos...! Headmistress, wait! I can... I think I can explain.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Melinoë! Where are we? One instant I discovered that our enemy invaded Erebus, then next I know he's groveling about my feet! How is this possible? What have you done...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, you... you don't remember... because you weren't there with me. But as for you, Chronos! Who am I now to you...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: You are... my granddaughter... Melinoë... whom I know both from this life, and an altogether different one... which I now realize... was but a dream. A delusion! So then... all I have left... is this?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: All that you see is what you wrought! As for this other life... how much of it do you recall?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Your youth, here in this House, with me. How you delighted in a hiding-game, when you were but a girl! How you and Zagreus would fight, but out of love... not in the way we fought this very night. I did not realize there could have been this other past we shared... this better past.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: A better past...? Nonsense! None of it occurred! Nothing has changed! You should be gone, Titan...! Not flooded with false memories!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...All these memories that sprang to his mind... I remember them as well. A life I could have lived... had the Titan never struck.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: But I struck! Ever-hated and shunned! Finish what you set your mind to, Granddaughter! For I have no desire to exist in such a world, with all of you, like this! Not for one moment longer...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Silence! Melinoë, come to your senses. Whatever you did, this wretch is finished, and shan't be using his old tricks again! End him!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Wait, please, let me prove this to you...! Chronos! Grandfather. Listen to me: Steady yourself. Use what power you yet have, and release our family from your hold!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: <Gasp> Our family...? They... why, they are bound as yet! I had them bound... for all this time. What have I done...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: My family...! Where are we...? Is this... my House? Father...! What is the meaning of all this?!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Hades... perhaps the Princess can explain. As for the Queen, and Prince, and all the rest... Melinoë, they need but hear your voice. And I await their judgment, and your own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Selene_01
dialogueLines.text: ...If I may briefly interrupt, we have arrived at our initial destination, everyone. Olympus is not far from here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I do appreciate the ride upon your gleaming chariot, O Moon; but it is time for me to go. Perhaps my conversations with Hades have prepared me to face my other sons...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We've informed Lord Uncle Zeus of what happened, and given our recommendations, though he'll have many questions for you. And ought to be expecting you by now. Remember the chance you've been given, Grandfather.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Time cannot forget, and ought never to be late. May Zeus at least permit me to assist you from here on, knowing there were real possibilities this all could have gone worse.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: To think that the entire flow of Time has fractured because of what occurred...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: I would liken it more to a moment of self-discovery on my part! Despite my advanced age, it seems I did not know all that there is to know about myself. But then again... who does? Now, farewell. And thank you, Granddaughter.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Apollo! And Lady Artemis!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: It's a beautiful night, Lady Selene! And you must be the esteemed Witch of the Crossroads! You've trained my cousin very well indeed, to have brought Chronos to our heights, still in one piece!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Fine, Apollo, I was incorrect for once, no need to boast. Melinoë, I may have wagered that you'd cut the Titan to ribbons sooner than you'd bring him here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know it's unimaginable, but he came here willingly. And Lord Zeus wanted to speak to him in private. Wait, does that mean... you were kicked out of your house?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Well we consider it a Palace, but, erm... no! No, we've been appointed to escort you to the Crossroads as a symbol of our family's unity, and come bearing a gift. Shall we be off?
